title: Aureline cited codebase explainer packet, claim row, citation, omission ledger, and open-evidence action contract
description: Boundary schema for the cited codebase explainer family. Every codebase-explainer surface (review-pack explainer overlay, AI evidence-explainer panel, support-bundle explainer packet, public query-family explainer, navigation-deep-link explainer, IDE inline-explainer overlay) renders one codebase_explainer_packet_record from this contract; each claim inside is one claim_row_record, each citation is one citation_ref_entry, and each disclosed gap is one omission_ledger_entry. Every claim is either backed by one or more citations that reuse the workspace_graph_seed identity layer (graph_node_record.node_id, graph_edge_record.edge_id, explainer_citation_slot.citation_ref) verbatim or is explicitly typed as omitted / downgraded / AI-assisted in the claim_disposition vocabulary, so unsupported claims cannot appear as authoritative prose. The omission ledger is first-class and carries typed omission_class entries (outside_active_workset, hidden_by_policy_projection, imported_root_unverified, evidence_missing_anchor_deleted, downgraded_refusal_insufficient_evidence, ai_inference_segment_excluded, ...) so a partial result is honest about what it left out instead of compressing into generic 'partial results' wording. Every export hook declares preserves_omission_ledger, preserves_citation_anchors, preserves_confidence_chip, preserves_imported_root_chip, preserves_anchor_drift_chip, and preserves_ai_inference_segment_chip explicitly so review-pack / support-bundle / AI-evidence packet exports cannot quietly compress the ledger or strip the typed chips. Open-evidence actions reuse stable graph and source-anchor ids (open_source_at_anchor, open_docs_at_citation, inspect_graph_relation, export_explainer_packet, flag_missing_evidence) instead of launching ambiguous searches; raw absolute paths, raw provider URLs, raw email addresses, and raw credentials never cross this boundary. Adding a new claim_class, claim_disposition, omission_class, offered_action_class, export_format_class, citation_subject_kind, or hidden-count axis is additive-minor and bumps codebase_explainer_packet_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

codebase_explainer_packet_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the codebase-explainer packet vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque equality-comparable string.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Never carries raw source bodies or raw URLs.

redaction_aware_text.description: Redaction-aware short text (≤ 1200 graphemes). Surfaces render this verbatim. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, raw email addresses, or raw credentials. Larger budget than the topology / impact strip note budget so a short cited-explainer claim sentence fits.

freshness_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#freshness. The freshness/provenance strip and per-citation entries carry one freshness class each.

stale_reason.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#stale_reason. Required when freshness_class != authoritative.

extraction_or_inference_mode.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#extraction_or_inference_mode. Surfaces never silently upgrade an ai_inference-derived row.

rolled_up_confidence.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/confidence_rollup.schema.json#confidence_level. Claims and citations render the rolled-up level verbatim.

floor_reason.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/confidence_rollup.schema.json#floor_reason. Each claim renders the typed floor reason alongside rolled_up_confidence so a reviewer can trace why the level landed there.

imported_root_status.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#imported_root_status. Imported citations carry the chip verbatim instead of folding into a local row.

scope_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#workset_scope_ref.scope_class. The active scope is named once on the packet header.

loaded_scope_state.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/topology_map_view.schema.json#loaded_scope_state. The packet never claims fully_loaded when the active workset, the policy projection, an imported-only contributor, or a missing-evidence condition narrowed the result set.

explained_subject_kind.description: What the explainer packet is about. The packet reuses the seed's identity layer for the subject; an explainer over a query reuses the query-family id.

subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the subject id. Reuses graph_node_record.node_id, graph_edge_record.edge_id, query-family id, topology view id, impact view id, or ownership card id verbatim. The packet never mints a private subject id.

subject_label.description: Optional redaction-aware label rendered alongside subject_ref. Never substituted on links or citations.

explained_subject.description: What the packet explains. Reuses the upstream identity layer rather than minting a private subject id.

claim_class.description: Frozen claim-class vocabulary. Each claim row names exactly one class so reviewers can dispatch on it without parsing prose. describes_subject: a property of the subject node / edge / query (kind, name, location). describes_relation: a typed graph relation (depends_on, owned_by, references_symbol, etc.). describes_lineage: a generated-artifact lineage relation. describes_ownership: a CODEOWNERS / team / rotation assignment. describes_impact: an impacts edge or impact-explorer row contribution. describes_imported_root: an imported-root membership or imported-bundle attribution. describes_anchor_drift: a typed source-anchor drift event. describes_missing_evidence: a missing-anchor / missing-lineage / dropped-citation case. describes_workset_scope: how the active workset / scope narrowed the answer. describes_runtime_role: a runtime / deployment / hosting role. describes_dependency: a depends_on relation drawn from a lockfile or package resolver. describes_generated_artifact: a build / codegen output. describes_policy_projection: a policy-limited view's effect. describes_ai_inference_segment: an explicitly AI-assisted narrative segment. describes_refusal: an explicit refusal to assert a claim because evidence is insufficient.

claim_disposition.description: Frozen claim-disposition vocabulary. Every claim row carries exactly one disposition so a reviewer can tell at a glance whether the claim is authoritatively cited, imported-only, inferred, AI-assisted narrative, a typed downgrade / refusal, or an omission carried for completeness. cited_direct_evidence: claim is backed by ≥ 1 direct citations from local-authoritative producers. cited_partial_imported: at least one citation is imported (imported_root_status != not_imported); confidence caps at medium. cited_partial_inferred: at least one citation is from heuristic / inferred relation; confidence caps at medium. cited_with_anchor_drift: at least one citation has anchor_drift_state != anchor_present_no_drift; the drift chip rides the row. cited_outside_active_workset: claim is cited but the citation lies outside the active workset / scope; the packet discloses the scope narrowing on the row. narrative_ai_assisted: an AI-inference pass produced narrative prose; the row caps at medium and renders the AI-inference chip; the row MUST list the supporting ai_evidence_packet ref. downgraded_refusal_insufficient_evidence: the producer refused to assert a strong claim because citations are insufficient; the row carries no authoritative claim text and the omission ledger names why. downgraded_refusal_policy_blocked / downgraded_refusal_evidence_missing: typed refusal variants. omitted_*: the claim was not asserted at all; the row is a placeholder so the omission ledger and the export packet do not lose track of the gap.

citation_subject_kind.description: What the citation points at. The packet reuses the seed's identity layer; a citation never mints a private pointer.

citation_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#explainer_citation_slot.citation_class. Cited evidence reuses this vocabulary verbatim instead of inventing explainer-only citation labels.

citation_id.description: Stable per-packet citation id. Open-evidence actions resolve back to this id so a reviewer can deep-link without re-deriving identity.

citation_ref.description: Opaque ref to the citation's underlying record (node_id, edge_id, explainer_citation_slot citation_ref, source_anchor opaque id, supporting_artifact_ref id, or upstream view id). Reused verbatim from the seed; never minted privately.

node_id_ref.description: Optional graph_node_record.node_id the citation lives on. Open-evidence actions resolve through this id.

edge_id_ref.description: Optional graph_edge_record.edge_id the citation lives on.

source_anchor_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the source_anchor_slot the citation points at. Reuses schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#source_anchor_slot identity verbatim. open_source_at_anchor resolves through this ref instead of launching an ambiguous search.

line_range.description: Optional line-range hint ('line_start:line_end') drawn from the source_anchor_slot. Raw source bytes never appear here.

anchor_drift_record_ref.description: Optional ref to a source_anchor_drift_record (schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json) when anchor_drift_state != anchor_present_no_drift.

confidence_rollup_ref.description: Optional ref to the confidence_rollup_record (schemas/graph/confidence_rollup.schema.json) that produced rolled_up_confidence for this citation.

provenance_stamp_ref.description: Required ref to the provenance_stamp_record (schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json) the citation resolves to. The same stamp every other surface walks; explainers never invent their own stamp.

citation_note.description: Optional redaction-aware note rendered alongside the citation. Raw source bodies, raw URLs, raw provider URLs never cross.

allOf.description: anchor_drift_state != anchor_present_no_drift MUST set anchor_drift_record_ref to a non-null opaque ref so the drift event remains auditable.
freshness_class != authoritative MUST carry a typed stale_reason.

citation_ref_entry.description: One citation entry inside a claim row. Reuses the seed's explainer_citation_slot, source_anchor_slot, and graph node / edge identity verbatim and pairs it with the provenance / confidence / anchor-drift / imported-root chips every other graph-backed surface renders. open_source_at_anchor and open_docs_at_citation resolve through this entry so the action is never an ambiguous search.

claim_id.description: Stable per-packet claim id. Open-evidence actions, copy-id actions, and review handoffs resolve through this id.

claim_text.description: Redaction-aware claim sentence the surface renders verbatim. Surfaces never replace this with a generic 'partial results' phrase. For downgraded_refusal_* and omitted_* dispositions the text MUST name the refusal / omission instead of asserting the underlying claim.

cited_node_id_refs.description: Optional list of graph_node_record.node_id refs the claim covers. Reused verbatim from the seed.

cited_edge_id_refs.description: Optional list of graph_edge_record.edge_id refs the claim covers. Reused verbatim from the seed.

confidence_rollup_ref.description: Optional ref to a confidence_rollup_record (surface_class explainer_citation) covering the per-claim rollup.

citation_refs.description: Citation entries backing the claim. Cited dispositions MUST carry minItems 1; downgraded_refusal_* and omitted_* dispositions MAY carry an empty array (the omission ledger names why).

ai_inference_segment.description: True when the claim text was produced or amended by an AI inference pass. narrative_ai_assisted MUST set this true; cited_* dispositions MUST set this false. Surfaces render the typed AI-inference chip instead of folding the segment into authoritative prose.

ai_evidence_packet_ref.description: Required non-null when ai_inference_segment is true; the ref points at the upstream ai_evidence_packet so a reviewer can replay the assistance segment.

omission_ledger_entry_ref.description: Required non-null when claim_disposition is one of downgraded_refusal_* or omitted_*; points at the omission_ledger_entry that records the reason. The ledger never disappears for a downgraded or omitted row.

supporting_artifact_refs.description: Zero or more supporting-artifact references the claim relies on. Support bundles, review packs, and AI evidence packets walk this list.

allOf.description: cited_* dispositions MUST carry at least one citation_ref. Downgraded refusal and omitted dispositions MAY carry zero citations (the ledger names why).
cited_partial_imported MUST list at least one citation whose imported_root_status != not_imported.
cited_with_anchor_drift MUST list at least one citation whose anchor_drift_state != anchor_present_no_drift.
narrative_ai_assisted MUST set ai_inference_segment true and MUST carry a non-null ai_evidence_packet_ref. Confidence caps at medium under ai_inference_caps_at_medium.

ai_inference_segment true MUST set narrative_ai_assisted disposition (or omitted_ai_inference_segment_redacted) and MUST carry a non-null ai_evidence_packet_ref so the AI-inference chip resolves to the upstream packet instead of disappearing.
cited_* dispositions MUST set ai_inference_segment false; cited evidence is not narrative AI prose.
downgraded_refusal_* and omitted_* dispositions MUST cap confidence at medium and carry a non-null omission_ledger_entry_ref so the gap is reviewable.

claim_row_record.description: One claim row. Every claim is either cited (one or more citation_refs whose ids reuse the seed's identity layer verbatim) or explicitly typed as downgraded / omitted / AI-assisted in claim_disposition; unsupported claims cannot appear as authoritative prose because the cited_* dispositions are gated on minItems-1 citation_refs and the downgraded / omitted dispositions are gated on a non-null omission_ledger_entry_ref.

omission_class.description: Frozen omission-class vocabulary. Every omission ledger entry names exactly one class so a partial result is honest about what it left out instead of compressing into generic 'partial results' wording. The vocabulary covers the spec's required disclosures: imported roots that were not cross-validated, outside-workset facts, unavailable edges (deleted / drifted / pending lineage), and AI-assisted segments that were excluded or redacted.

omission_id.description: Stable per-packet omission id. claim_row_record.omission_ledger_entry_ref points at this id; flag_missing_evidence and copy_omission_id actions resolve through it.

omission_text.description: Redaction-aware sentence the surface renders verbatim. Surfaces MUST NOT compress this into a generic 'partial results' phrase; the omission_class names which axis was lost and the text names the specifics.

referenced_node_id_refs.description: Optional list of node_id refs the omission applies to. The exact list MAY be empty when the count is known but the ids are policy-hidden; subject_count carries the authoritative number.

referenced_edge_id_refs.description: Optional list of edge_id refs the omission applies to.

subject_count_known.description: True when the omission's authoritative subject count is known. False when the producer cannot enumerate (warming index, missing lineage).

subject_count.description: Number of subjects this omission covers when subject_count_known is true. Null otherwise.

imported_root_status.description: Optional imported_root_status when the omission is an imported-root case (imported_root_unverified, imported_root_not_loaded). Reused verbatim from the seed.

anchor_drift_state.description: Optional anchor_drift_state when the omission is an evidence_missing_* case driven by anchor drift.

anchor_drift_record_ref.description: Optional ref to a source_anchor_drift_record when anchor_drift_state is set and not anchor_present_no_drift.

policy_view_ref.description: Optional ref to a policy_view_node when the omission is hidden_by_policy_projection. The hidden subject ids never project; the policy view ref does.

supporting_artifact_refs.description: Zero or more supporting-artifact refs that explain why the omission applies. Support bundles and review packs walk this list.

export_preservation_required.description: True when this omission MUST be carried verbatim through review-pack / support-bundle / AI-evidence packet exports. The packet's offered_export_hooks MUST set preserves_omission_ledger true on at least one entry; the ledger is never compressed into a generic 'partial results' phrase on export.

allOf.description: subject_count_known true MUST carry a non-null integer count; subject_count_known false MUST carry null.
anchor_drift_state non-null and non-anchor_present_no_drift MUST carry a non-null anchor_drift_record_ref.
hidden_by_policy_projection MUST carry a non-null policy_view_ref so the policy-side review handoff resolves through one ref.
ai_inference_segment_excluded and ai_inference_segment_redacted MUST set export_preservation_required true so the AI-assisted gap survives review-pack / support-bundle export.
downgraded_refusal_* omissions MUST set export_preservation_required true so the refusal survives review-pack / support-bundle export.

omission_ledger_entry.description: One omission ledger entry. The ledger is the spec's required disclosure that imported roots, outside-workset facts, unavailable edges, and AI-assisted excluded segments do not silently disappear; the omission_class is closed and the export hooks carry the ledger verbatim through review-pack / support-bundle / AI-evidence packet exports.

freshness_provenance_strip_entry.description: Mirrors schemas/graph/topology_map_view.schema.json#freshness_provenance_strip_entry; the explainer packet renders the same chip set every other graph-backed surface renders so an explainer cannot drop the freshness / confidence / anchor-drift / imported-root chips.

omission_known.description: True when the omission ledger is enumerated. False when the producer cannot enumerate (warming index, policy projection).

omission_count.description: Total count of omission_ledger_entry rows. Drives the panel header so 'N omitted axes disclosed' is rendered verbatim.

ai_inference_segment_count.description: Count of claim rows whose ai_inference_segment is true.

downgraded_refusal_count.description: Count of claim rows whose claim_disposition is one of downgraded_refusal_*.

hidden_count_summary.description: Hidden / partial / imported-root / omission / AI-inference / refusal accounting for the explainer packet. Counts cross the boundary; the hidden subject ids themselves never do (mirrors schemas/graph/topology_map_view.schema.json#hidden_count_summary plus the explainer-only omission, AI-inference, and downgraded-refusal axes).

offered_action_class.description: Frozen open-evidence action vocabulary. Every action resolves through the stable ids defined in this schema (packet_id, claim_id, citation_id, omission_id) or through the seed's node_id / edge_id / source_anchor / provenance_stamp / confidence_rollup / anchor_drift / topology view / impact view / ownership card identity, never through an ambiguous string search. The minimum set required by the spec is open_source_at_anchor, open_docs_at_citation, inspect_graph_relation, export_explainer_packet, and flag_missing_evidence; surfaces MAY offer the full set.

offered_action_target_kind.description: What kind of subject the action resolves against. The action_target_ref reuses the matching schema's id family verbatim.

action_target_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the action target. open_source_at_anchor resolves through a source_anchor_slot ref; inspect_graph_relation resolves through an edge_id; inspect_topology_view resolves through a topology_map_view_record view_id; etc.

export_format_class.description: Frozen export-format vocabulary for explainer packets. review-pack / support-bundle / AI-evidence / json / yaml exports preserve the omission ledger, the citation anchors, the confidence chip, the imported-root chip, the anchor-drift chip, and the AI-inference chip. markdown_explainer_brief and csv_claim_table are lossy and MUST disclose the loss verbatim on the export receipt.

preserves_omission_ledger.description: True when the export carries every omission_ledger_entry verbatim (omission_class, omission_text, subject_count, supporting_artifact_refs). Lossy formats MUST set this false; the surface MUST disclose the loss on the export receipt.

preserves_citation_anchors.description: True when the export preserves citation_ref / source_anchor_ref / line_range so open_source_at_anchor and open_docs_at_citation still resolve from the export.

preserves_confidence_chip.description: True when the export carries rolled_up_confidence + floor_reason + conflict_state per claim and per citation.

preserves_imported_root_chip.description: True when the export preserves the per-citation imported_root_status chip.

preserves_anchor_drift_chip.description: True when the export preserves the per-citation anchor_drift_state chip.

preserves_ai_inference_segment_chip.description: True when the export preserves the per-claim ai_inference_segment chip plus the ai_evidence_packet_ref. Surfaces never claim an export carries the chip if the format does not actually project it.

manifest_ref.description: Optional opaque manifest ref the export will be filed under (review pack id, support bundle id, AI evidence packet id).

review_handoff_class.description: Frozen review-handoff vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/graph/topology_map_view.schema.json and schemas/graph/impact_reason.schema.json so explainer, topology, and impact surfaces share one handoff vocabulary instead of inventing per-surface synonyms.

packet_id.description: Stable packet id. Open-evidence actions, copy_packet_id, and review handoffs resolve through this id.

workspace_graph_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the workspace_graph_record this packet was derived from.

topology_view_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to a topology_map_view_record. inspect_topology_view resolves through this ref.

impact_view_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to an impact_explorer_view_record. inspect_impact_view resolves through this ref.

ownership_card_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to an ownership_card_record. inspect_ownership_card resolves through this ref.

active_scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the active workset / scope artifact. The packet never mints a parallel scope id.

summary_text.description: Optional redaction-aware top-level summary. The summary MUST NOT introduce claims that are not also asserted (or omitted) on claim_rows; the row layer is the authoritative claim layer.

freshness_provenance_strip.description: One or more strip entries the packet renders. The strip is required so an explainer surface cannot drop the freshness / confidence / anchor-drift / imported-root chips that every other graph-backed surface carries.

claim_rows.description: One or more claim rows. Every claim is either cited (citation_refs minItems 1) or explicitly typed as downgraded / omitted / AI-assisted via claim_disposition; unsupported claims cannot appear as authoritative prose.

omission_ledger.description: Zero or more omission ledger entries. Empty array is admissible only when loaded_scope_state == fully_loaded and no claim row carries a downgraded_refusal_* / omitted_* / narrative_ai_assisted disposition; the schema's allOf rules enforce the invariants.

offered_actions.description: Offered open-evidence actions. Every packet offers at least the spec's minimum set: open_source_at_anchor, open_docs_at_citation, inspect_graph_relation, export_explainer_packet, flag_missing_evidence. Surfaces MAY offer the full vocabulary.

supporting_artifact_refs.description: Packet-level supporting artifact refs. Walked by review packs, support bundles, and AI evidence packets.

allOf.description: loaded_scope_state == fully_loaded MUST set every hidden_count_summary count field to 0 and every count_known field to true. The packet never claims completeness while disclosing hidden rows.
loaded_scope_state == imported_root_only_loaded MUST set hidden_count_summary.imported_root_count >= 1.
loaded_scope_state == policy_limited_loaded MUST set hidden_by_policy_known true.
Every packet's offered_actions MUST contain the spec's minimum action set: open_source_at_anchor, open_docs_at_citation, inspect_graph_relation, export_explainer_packet, flag_missing_evidence.

Whenever any claim row carries a downgraded_refusal_* or omitted_* disposition, the omission_ledger MUST be non-empty and at least one offered_export_hook MUST set preserves_omission_ledger true so the ledger survives export.
Whenever any claim row's ai_inference_segment is true, at least one offered_export_hook MUST set preserves_ai_inference_segment_chip true so the AI-inference chip survives export.
Whenever any claim row carries cited_partial_imported or any citation has imported_root_status != not_imported, at least one offered_export_hook MUST set preserves_imported_root_chip true.
Whenever any claim row carries cited_with_anchor_drift or any citation has anchor_drift_state != anchor_present_no_drift, at least one offered_export_hook MUST set preserves_anchor_drift_chip true.

codebase_explainer_packet_record.description: One cited codebase explainer packet. Every claim is either cited (citation_refs minItems 1, citation ids reuse the seed's identity layer verbatim) or explicitly typed as downgraded_refusal_* / omitted_* / narrative_ai_assisted with a non-null omission_ledger_entry_ref so unsupported claims cannot appear as authoritative prose. The omission_ledger is first-class and carries typed omission_class entries (outside_active_workset, imported_root_unverified, evidence_missing_anchor_deleted, downgraded_refusal_insufficient_evidence, ai_inference_segment_excluded, ...) so a partial result is honest about what it left out instead of compressing into generic 'partial results' wording. Open-evidence actions resolve through stable graph and source-anchor ids; offered_actions carries at least the spec's minimum set. Export hooks declare preserves_omission_ledger / preserves_citation_anchors / preserves_confidence_chip / preserves_imported_root_chip / preserves_anchor_drift_chip / preserves_ai_inference_segment_chip explicitly; review-pack / support-bundle / AI-evidence packet exports preserve the ledger and the typed chips.
